prompt:
Goal: Create a richly detailed vintage travel poster for Tianjin, styled like a hand-drawn scrapbook collage on aged paper, celebrating history, landmarks, food, and modern urban energy.

Canvas: Vertical 3:4 poster, warm beige parchment background with visible paper grain, creases, ink smudges, postal marks, taped stickers, torn paper edges, map fragments, and travel-diary annotations. Use a dense but readable composition with a nostalgic illustrated postcard look.

Main headline: At the very top, place huge bold red-orange Chinese brush lettering reading 天津, outlined in black and white, slightly rough and hand-painted. Beneath it, repeat smaller black calligraphy text “天津”. Add an English subtitle near the center-top: “A CITY OF HISTORY AND MODERN ENERGY”.

Central city scene: Fill the middle with a colorful hand-inked urban panorama. Include exactly 5 major landmark/scene elements: 1) the Tianjin Radio and Television Tower on the left with a small label “天津广播电视塔”, 2) a giant Ferris wheel over a river in the center labeled “天津之眼”, 3) a blue river with bridges, promenades, trees, and small boats labeled “海河风光”, 4) ornate European-style historic buildings on the right labeled “五大道 意式风情区”, and 5) a traditional decorative Chinese gate on the lower right with a blue plaque reading “津门故里” and a street behind it labeled “古文化街”. Add a sunny sky with blue watercolor wash, fluffy clouds, birds, and a simple orange sun on the upper right. Behind the historic buildings, add a few modern glass skyscrapers.

Foreground: Add exactly 3 prominent foreground travel/food elements: 1) a retro cream, red, and green tram facing forward on tracks in the lower center, with the Chinese characters “天 津” on its front and a label “意式风情区”; 2) a bamboo steamer with three white buns labeled “狗不理包子”; 3) a patterned bowl of noodles or savory snack labeled “煎饼果子”, plus a small cylindrical cup or container labeled “天津味道”.

Sticker and paper collage details: Around the borders, include exactly 10 decorative travel stickers or notes: 1) top-left yellow note “I LOVE 天津” with a red heart, 2) top-left round postal stamp “TIANJIN 天津”, 3) left blue note “CITY TRIP” with a heart, 4) upper-right pink ticket “EXPLORE” with a camera icon, 5) upper-right yellow note “DISCOVER 天津之美!” with a heart, 6) right-side white tag with map pin and Chinese text “海河之城 魅力天津”, 7) lower-left torn map sticker with a location pin and “TIANJIN”, 8) lower-left pink ticket “BEST VACATION”, 9) lower-left green note “BEST MEMORIES” with a small camera, and 10) lower-right purple ticket “TRAVEL” with an airplane icon. Also add two postal stamp impressions reading “TIANJIN 天津” near the left edge.

Bottom banner: Across the bottom, place a torn cream paper banner with hand lettering “WELCOME TO 天津”, followed by smaller text “EXPLORE HISTORY, ENJOY THE JOURNEY!” Decorate it with red hearts, doodled stars, arrows, little flowers, and tape marks.

Right checklist: Add a small yellow checklist note on the right side titled “打卡清单” with exactly 5 checked items: “天津之眼”, “五大道”, “古文化街”, “意式风情”, and “海河游船”.

Visual style: Hand-drawn watercolor and ink illustration, bright travel-journal palette of red, teal, sky blue, mustard yellow, warm cream, and black outlines. Use imperfect typography, sketchy outlines, rough brush strokes, stamped textures, layered paper cutouts, and cheerful tourist-poster energy. Keep the poster crowded and information-rich but balanced, like a nostalgic illustrated city guide.

Constraints: Preserve the Chinese place-name text where specified, keep all counted elements exactly as listed, avoid photorealism, avoid empty modern minimalism, no logos or watermarks.
